recipes.title: Jerk Chicken
recipes.description: Jamaica's iconic spice-marinated chicken slow-grilled over pimento wood — fiery, smoky, and aromatic with allspice and Scotch bonnet.
steps: Blend Scotch bonnets, scallions, garlic, thyme, allspice, soy sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, oil, ginger, nutmeg, and cinnamon into a thick paste.
Score chicken pieces deeply through the skin and slather the jerk paste all over and into the cuts.
Marinate at least 8 hours, ideally 24, in the refrigerator.
Build a charcoal fire and add pimento wood chips if available; heat should be medium-low for slow cooking.
Grill chicken skin-side up first, away from direct flame, with the lid down for 30 minutes.
Turn and cook another 25 minutes, basting with reserved marinade and moving over hotter spots to char the skin.
Internal temperature should reach 75°C. Rest 5 minutes, chop into smaller pieces, and serve with rice and peas.

recipes.title: Ackee and Saltfish
recipes.description: Jamaica's national breakfast: salted cod sautéed with ackee fruit, peppers, onions, and tomatoes — buttery and savory.
steps: Soak saltfish in cold water overnight, changing water 2–3 times to remove excess salt.
Boil saltfish for 15 minutes, drain, and flake with a fork, removing skin and bones.
If using canned ackee, drain very gently to keep pieces intact.
Heat oil and sauté onion, bell pepper, and scallions until soft, about 6 minutes.
Add garlic, thyme, Scotch bonnet, and tomato; cook 3 minutes until tomato softens.
Stir in flaked saltfish and black pepper, cook 2 minutes.
Gently fold in ackee and warm through 3 minutes — handle delicately so it doesn't break apart. Serve with fried dumplings or boiled green bananas.

recipes.title: Curry Goat
recipes.description: Jamaican curry goat slow-braised with curry powder, allspice, and Scotch bonnet — tender, fragrant, and unapologetically rich.
steps: Toss goat meat with curry powder, allspice, half the garlic, half the ginger, scallions, thyme, and salt. Marinate at least 4 hours.
Heat oil in a heavy pot until very hot. Brown the goat in batches, scraping up the toasted curry from the pan.
Add onion, remaining garlic, and ginger; cook 4 minutes.
Pour in water or stock, drop in the whole Scotch bonnet (do not pierce), and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 90 minutes — check occasionally and top up liquid if needed.
Add potato and continue cooking 30 minutes until potato is tender and goat is fork-tender.
Discard Scotch bonnet, adjust salt, and let the curry rest 10 minutes. Serve with rice and peas.

recipes.title: Oxtail Stew
recipes.description: Jamaican oxtail braised low and slow with butter beans, allspice, and brown sauce until the meat falls off the bone.
steps: Season oxtail with allspice, browning, soy, Worcestershire, half the garlic, ginger, scallions, thyme, salt, and pepper. Marinate 4 hours.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown oxtail on all sides — about 10 minutes total.
Add onion and remaining garlic; cook 3 minutes.
Pour in 1.5 liters water, drop in the whole Scotch bonnet (unpierced), bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 2.5 hours, topping up water as needed.
Add carrot and butter beans; simmer 30 minutes more until oxtail is fall-apart tender and sauce thickened.
Discard Scotch bonnet, adjust seasoning, and serve over white rice or rice and peas.

recipes.title: Jamaican Patties
recipes.description: Flaky golden turmeric pastries with spiced ground beef filling — Jamaica's most popular street snack.
steps: Combine flour, turmeric, curry powder, and salt. Cut in cold butter until pea-sized, then add ice water and form a dough. Chill 1 hour.
Brown beef with onion, scallions, garlic, Scotch bonnet, thyme, and allspice. Season well with salt.
Stir in breadcrumbs and 80 ml water; simmer 5 minutes until thick. Cool completely.
Roll dough thin and cut into 15 cm circles.
Spoon filling onto half of each circle, fold over into half-moons, and crimp edges firmly with a fork.
Brush tops with beaten egg.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 25 minutes until deeply golden and flaky.

recipes.title: Rice and Peas
recipes.description: Jamaican kidney beans and rice cooked together with coconut milk, thyme, and Scotch bonnet — Sunday dinner's heart.
steps: Drain soaked beans and place in a pot with 1 liter fresh water. Boil 50–60 minutes until tender. Reserve cooking liquid.
To the beans, add coconut milk, scallions, garlic, thyme, allspice, Scotch bonnet (do not pierce), and salt.
Bring to a boil and adjust seasoning.
Add rice and enough reserved bean liquid so the total liquid is about 1.5 times the volume of rice.
Bring back to a boil, then immediately reduce to lowest heat and cover tightly.
Cook 18 minutes without lifting the lid.
Discard Scotch bonnet, fluff with a fork, and rest 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Escovitch Fish
recipes.description: Crisp-fried whole fish topped with a tangy pickle of vinegar, peppers, carrots, and onion — a Jamaican Easter staple.

ingredients.name: whole snapper or grunt, scaled and gutted

steps: Score fish on both sides, rub with lime juice, salt, and pepper. Rest 15 minutes.
Pat fish dry and dredge lightly in flour.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry fish 6–7 minutes per side until skin is crisp and meat cooked through. Drain on paper towels.
In a saucepan, combine vinegar, onion, carrot, bell pepper, Scotch bonnet, pimento berries, and a pinch of salt.
Bring to a boil and simmer 3 minutes — vegetables should remain crunchy.
Place fish on a serving plate.
Pour the hot pickle and its liquid over the fish and let stand 15 minutes for flavors to meld. Serve at room temperature with bammy or hard dough bread.

recipes.title: Bammy
recipes.description: Jamaican flatbread made from grated cassava, soaked in coconut milk and pan-fried — a crisp, slightly sweet side for fish.
steps: Finely grate the peeled cassava on a fine grater into a bowl.
Place grated cassava in a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much liquid as possible — this is critical.
Mix the dry cassava with salt.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Place a metal ring (about 12 cm) in the pan.
Press a portion of cassava firmly into the ring to about 1 cm thick. Cook 7 minutes until the bottom sets and lightly browns.
Remove ring, flip, and cook 5 more minutes. Repeat with remaining cassava.
Soak cooked bammies briefly in coconut milk, then pan-fry in oil 2 minutes per side until golden. Serve with escovitch fish.

recipes.title: Brown Stew Chicken
recipes.description: Jamaican chicken pieces marinated with herbs and seared, then simmered in a rich brown gravy of allspice, soy, and tomato.
steps: Marinate chicken pieces with browning sauce, soy sauce, allspice, half the garlic, half the scallions, thyme, salt, and pepper for 2 hours.
Heat oil in a heavy pot until very hot. Brown chicken pieces in batches until deeply colored on all sides.
Remove chicken and pour off most of the oil.
Sauté onion, remaining scallions, garlic, ginger, and bell pepper for 4 minutes.
Stir in tomato, tomato paste, and 500 ml water. Bring to a boil.
Return chicken with any juices, drop in Scotch bonnet (do not pierce), cover, and simmer 30 minutes.
Add carrot and cook 10 minutes more until chicken is tender and gravy is glossy. Discard Scotch bonnet and serve over rice.

recipes.title: Rundown
recipes.description: Jamaican mackerel simmered in reduced coconut milk with onions, peppers, and thyme until thick and luscious.
steps: If using salted mackerel, soak in cold water 4 hours, drain, and rinse.
Pour coconut milk into a wide pan and simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes until reduced and oil starts to separate.
Add onion, scallions, garlic, ginger, allspice, and thyme; cook 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato and Scotch bonnet (whole, not pierced); simmer 5 minutes.
Add mackerel pieces and lime juice, gently spooning sauce over the fish.
Simmer 10–12 minutes uncovered until fish is cooked and the sauce is very thick.
Discard Scotch bonnet, adjust salt, and serve with boiled green bananas, dumplings, or yam.

recipes.title: Cornmeal Porridge
recipes.description: Jamaican breakfast porridge of cornmeal cooked with coconut milk, cinnamon, and nutmeg, sweetened with condensed milk.
steps: In a bowl, whisk cornmeal with 250 ml of the cold water until smooth — this prevents lumps.
Bring remaining water to a boil with cinnamon stick, nutmeg, and salt.
Slowly drizzle the cornmeal slurry into the boiling water, whisking constantly.
Reduce heat to low and cook 12 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent sticking, until thickened and the cornmeal taste is gone.
Stir in coconut milk and cook another 5 minutes until creamy.
Off heat, stir in vanilla extract and sweetened condensed milk to taste.
Discard cinnamon stick, ladle into bowls, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Festival
recipes.description: Sweet Jamaican fried cornmeal dumplings — golden, slightly crisp outside and tender inside, served alongside jerk and fried fish.
steps: Whisk together flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg.
Stir in vanilla and gradually add water, mixing into a soft, slightly tacky dough.
Knead briefly on a floured surface until smooth, then rest 10 minutes covered.
Divide dough into 8 equal portions and roll each into a 12 cm long log, tapered at the ends.
Heat oil to 170°C (340°F) — too hot and the outside will burn before the inside cooks.
Fry festivals in batches, turning frequently, for 6–7 minutes until deeply golden all over.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot alongside jerk chicken or escovitch fish.
